dissection.title: DOES GENDER MODERATE THE ASSOCIATION BETWEEN PSYCHOPATHIC PERSONALITY TRAITS AND EXTERNALIZING BEHAVIORS? A META-ANALYSIS

constructs.evidence: Psychopathic traits are a strong predictor of maladaptive socioemotional and behavioral outcomes
the correlation between psychopathic traits and externalizing would be greater in males compared to females across all types of externalizing except for social aggression
Triarchic disinhibition was a better predictor of non-violent problematic behavior in men compared to women
triarchic meanness was a better predictor of violence and aggression in men compared to women

hypotheses.text: The correlation between psychopathic traits and externalizing would be greater in males compared to females
hypotheses.detail: across all types of externalizing except for social aggression
hypotheses.evidence: I hypothesized that the correlation between psychopathic traits and externalizing would be greater in males compared to females across all types of externalizing except for social aggression

design.evidence: The current meta-analysis examined whether gender moderates the relation between psychopathic traits and externalizing behaviors

sample.evidence: A total of 79 references (k = 83 reports) were included in the analyses

analysis.evidence: Male and female correlations between psychopathic traits and externalizing were transformed into z scores

data.evidence: The PsycINFO database was used to identify eligible studies

findings.text: Most psychopathic traits are associated with externalizing behaviors in both men and women
findings.evidence: There were consistent associations between most psychopathic traits and externalizing behaviors for both men and women

findings.text: Only two significant effects for gender differences
findings.evidence: Of 39 comparisons, only two yielded significant effects
